commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `intentionalDisconnect` flag to Discord gateway close handling and regression tests for abnormal 1006 recovery versus intentional disconnect suppression.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +50. Total +60 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? No: the PR’s test can reproduce the proposed stale private state in source, but current main inspection did not prove the same `shouldReconnect=false` abnormal non-intentional close path is reachable in a real Discord run.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the remaining blocker is real production proof and owner judgment about the reachable SUP-2240 stale-state path, not a narrow code repair ClawSweeper can safely infer.

target.title: fix(discord): reconnect after abnormal gateway close
target.body:
## Summary
- re-arm Discord gateway reconnect after abnormal non-intentional closes when shouldReconnect had been cleared
- preserve intentional disconnect and normal close behavior
- add regression coverage for abnormal 1006 recovery and intentional disconnect suppression

## Verification
- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/internal/gateway.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts

## Ops note
- Do not hot-patch or restart the live /usr/local OpenClaw gateway for this PR.
- Live runtime adoption should happen through the normal reviewed release/upgrade path after merge.
- Paperclip: SUP-2240
target.author: levineam
